name: portspoof
title: portspoof

commands.name: portspoof

arguments.description: ip : Bind to a particular  IP address
arguments.raw: -i			  ip : Bind to a particular  IP address

arguments.description: port : Bind to a particular PORT number
arguments.raw: -p			  port : Bind to a particular PORT number

arguments.description: file_path : Portspoof service signature regex. file
arguments.raw: -s			  file_path : Portspoof service signature regex. file

arguments.description: file_path : Log port scanning alerts to a file
arguments.raw: -l			  file_path : Log port scanning alerts to a file

arguments.description: file_path : FUZZER_MODE - fuzzing payload file list
arguments.raw: -f			  file_path : FUZZER_MODE - fuzzing payload file list

arguments.description: file_path : FUZZER_MODE - wrapping signatures file list
arguments.raw: -n			  file_path : FUZZER_MODE - wrapping signatures file list

arguments.description: switch to simple reply mode (doesn't work for Nmap)!
arguments.raw: -2			  switch to simple reply mode (doesn't work for Nmap)!

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# portspoof --help
 Try ` portspoof -h' for more information.

local_help.-h:
Usage: portspoof [OPTION]...
Portspoof - service emulator / frontend exploitation framework.

-i			  ip : Bind to a particular  IP address
-p			  port : Bind to a particular PORT number
-s			  file_path : Portspoof service signature regex. file
-c			  file_path : Portspoof configuration file
-l			  file_path : Log port scanning alerts to a file
-f			  file_path : FUZZER_MODE - fuzzing payload file list 
-n			  file_path : FUZZER_MODE - wrapping signatures file list
-1			  FUZZER_MODE - generate fuzzing payloads internally
-2			  switch to simple reply mode (doesn't work for Nmap)!
-D			  run as daemon process
-d			  disable syslog
-v			  be verbose
-h			  display this help and exit

binary_metadata.file_type: a /bin/env sh script, ASCII text executable

apt_metadata.description:
enhance OS security through a set of techniques
This package contains a service to enhance OS security through a set of
following techniques:
* All 65535 TCP ports are always open
Instead of informing an attacker that a particular port is in a CLOSED or
FILTERED state Portspoof will return SYN+ACK for every port connection
attempt/
* Every open TCP port emulates a service
Portspoof has a huge database of dynamic service signatures, that will be
used to generate fake banners and fool scanners.
.
This tool requires configuration before use.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use portspoof because ### portspoof.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `portspoof [OPTION]...` as documented upstream for portspoof.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair portspoof with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `portspoof --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install portspoof. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `portspoof [OPTION]...`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### portspoof.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install portspoof
Install with: sudo apt install portspoof
